questions.question: Solve |x| = 7.
questions.explanation: The absolute value of x equals 7 when x is 7 units from 0 on the number line. This happens at both x = 7 and x = -7.

questions.question: Solve |x - 3| = 5.
questions.explanation: The distance from x to 3 is 5, so x - 3 = 5 or x - 3 = -5. Solving gives x = 8 or x = -2.

questions.question: Solve |2x| = 10.
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 2 to get |x| = 5. Then x = 5 or x = -5.

questions.question: For what values of x is |x + 4| ≤ 2 true?
questions.explanation: The expression |x + 4| ≤ 2 means -2 ≤ x + 4 ≤ 2. Subtract 4 from all parts: -6 ≤ x ≤ -2.

questions.question: A temperature reading has an error of |t - 72| = 3 degrees. What are the possible actual temperatures?
questions.explanation: The absolute difference between the actual temperature t and 72 is 3, so t - 72 = 3 or t - 72 = -3. This gives t = 75 or t = 69.

questions.question: Solve |x| > 4.
questions.explanation: The absolute value of x is greater than 4 when x is more than 4 units away from 0. This occurs when x is less than -4 or greater than 4.
